NSAIDs.mechanism: Reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis, causing afferent arteriole vasoconstriction and reduced GFR

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.dose_adjustment: Avoid if eGFR <30; use with caution 30-60

drugs.name: Naproxen
drugs.dose_adjustment: Avoid if eGFR <30

drugs.name: Diclofenac
drugs.dose_adjustment: Avoid if eGFR <30

drugs.name: Indomethacin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Avoid if eGFR <30

drugs.name: Ketorolac
drugs.dose_adjustment: Contraindicated in advanced CKD

drugs.name: Celecoxib
drugs.dose_adjustment: Use with caution; avoid <30

Aminoglycosides.mechanism: Direct tubular toxicity causing acute tubular necrosis

drugs.name: Gentamicin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Extend dosing interval based on eGFR; monitor levels closely

drugs.name: Tobramycin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Dose reduction required; calculate by Cockcroft-Gault

drugs.name: Amikacin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Significant dose reduction; peak/trough monitoring essential

drugs.name: Streptomycin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Reduce dose and extend interval

Contrast_Agents.mechanism: Contrast-induced nephropathy via direct tubular toxicity and renal vasoconstriction

drugs.name: Iodinated contrast (iohexol)
drugs.dose_adjustment: Hold metformin; pre-hydration required; minimize dose

drugs.name: Gadolinium (MRI)
drugs.dose_adjustment: Risk of nephrogenic systemic fibrosis; avoid if eGFR <30

Antibiotics.mechanism: Various mechanisms including direct tubulotoxicity and interstitial nephritis

drugs.name: Vancomycin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Dose by AUC monitoring; significant accumulation in CKD

drugs.name: Amphotericin B
drugs.dose_adjustment: Use liposomal formulation; monitor renal function daily

drugs.name: Acyclovir
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR 25-50: 50% dose; eGFR 10-25: 50% dose q24h; <10: 50% dose q48h

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR <30: 50% dose or extend interval

drugs.name: Nitrofurantoin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Contraindicated if eGFR <30 (ineffective and toxic metabolite accumulation)

ACE_Inhibitors.mechanism: Can reduce GFR acutely (especially with bilateral RAS); monitor K+ and creatinine

drugs.name: Lisinopril
drugs.dose_adjustment: Start low; monitor K+ and creatinine at 1-2 weeks; acceptable if stable

drugs.name: Enalapril
drugs.dose_adjustment: Reduce dose if eGFR <30; beneficial for proteinuria

drugs.name: Ramipril
drugs.dose_adjustment: Renoprotective; dose reduce below eGFR 30

drugs.name: Benazepril
drugs.dose_adjustment: Beneficial in proteinuric CKD; monitor K+

Diuretics.mechanism: Volume depletion can precipitate AKI; electrolyte disturbances

drugs.name: Furosemide
drugs.dose_adjustment: Higher doses needed as GFR falls; monitor electrolytes

drugs.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
drugs.dose_adjustment: Ineffective below eGFR 30; switch to loop diuretic

drugs.name: Spironolactone
drugs.dose_adjustment: Risk of hyperkalemia; avoid eGFR <30

Diabetes_Medications.mechanism: Accumulation of parent drug or metabolites causing toxicity or hypoglycemia

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR 30-45: 50% dose; eGFR <30: contraindicated (lactic acidosis risk)

drugs.name: Sitagliptin
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR 30-45: 50mg/day; eGFR <30: 25mg/day

drugs.name: Empagliflozin
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR <45: reduced efficacy; eGFR <20: avoid for glycemic control

drugs.name: Glibenclamide
drugs.dose_adjustment: Avoid in CKD; active metabolite accumulates causing hypoglycemia

Chemotherapy.mechanism: Direct nephrotoxicity; cisplatin causes proximal tubular injury

drugs.name: Cisplatin
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR 40-50: 75% dose; <40: avoid; aggressive hydration required

drugs.name: Carboplatin
drugs.dose_adjustment: Dose by Calvert formula using GFR

drugs.name: Methotrexate
drugs.dose_adjustment: Significant dose reduction required; toxic below eGFR 30

Immunosuppressants.mechanism: Calcineurin inhibitors cause afferent arteriole vasoconstriction (acute) and tubulointerstitial fibrosis (chronic)

drugs.name: Cyclosporine
drugs.dose_adjustment: Monitor levels closely; nephrotoxicity dose-dependent

drugs.name: Tacrolimus
drugs.dose_adjustment: Monitor trough levels; reduce dose for toxicity

drugs.name: Lithium
drugs.dose_adjustment: eGFR <60: reduce dose; monitor levels; can cause nephrogenic DI

NSAIDs: Acetaminophen (paracetamol) preferred for pain; tramadol with caution; low-dose opioids for severe pain
Contrast_Agents: CO2 angiography; MRI without contrast; ultrasound; pre-hydration protocols
Antibiotics: Beta-lactams generally safer; dose-adjust all renally-cleared antibiotics
